title: Carbon Accounting Software Comparison: Carbon Trust vs GHG Protocol Tools vs Emitwise for Small Businesses - 2026 Review
description: In-depth comparison of Carbon Trust, GHG Protocol Tools, and Emitwise carbon accounting software. Analyze accounting standards, data collection, and report
content:
Background on Carbon Accounting Software

In 2026, carbon accounting has become a fundamental capability for corporate sustainability. Whether meeting regulatory carbon emission reporting requirements or applying for carbon neutral certification, you need accurate carbon emission accounting data. Small and medium-sized businesses face three main pain points in carbon accounting: complex accounting standards (Scope 1, Scope 2, Scope 3 classifications), difficult emission factor selection (different energy types, different regional variations), and multiple report format requirements (regulatory, certification, investor needs). Professional carbon accounting software provides built-in accounting standards, automated data collection, and multi-format report generation to help businesses reduce accounting costs, improve data accuracy, and meet multiple compliance needs. This article reviews three mainstream carbon accounting software options to guide small businesses in their selection.

Core Feature Comparison Table

FeatureCarbon TrustGHG Protocol ToolsEmitwiseSmall Business Fit
Accounting StandardsISO14064+GHG ProtocolGHG Protocol officialISO14064+GHG ProtocolAll platforms cover standards
Scope CoverageScope1+2+3 completeScope1+2 mainlyScope1+2+3 completeEmitwise covers most
Data CollectionAPI integration+Excel importExcel templates mainlyAPI integration+supply chain collectionEmitwise most automated
Emission Factor LibraryGlobal emission factorsOfficial emission factorsGlobal+China emission factorsEmitwise China-friendly

Carbon Trust Carbon Accounting Platform Details

Carbon Trust is a UK government-supported carbon management organization, and its carbon accounting software emphasizes Carbon Trust carbon neutral certification connection.

  • Core Capabilities: ISO14064+GHG Protocol accounting standards built-in; Scope 1, Scope 2, Scope 3 complete coverage; API integration with enterprise ERP systems to automatically capture energy consumption data; Global emission factor library covering 200+ national energy emission factors; Carbon Trust carbon neutral certification direct connection, accounting data directly used for certification applications.
  • Technical Advantages: Mature Carbon Trust certification connection, one-stop certification application completion; Rich global emission factor library covering 200+ national energy types; Complete Scope 3 accounting coverage, supply chain carbon emission penetration; Strong brand authority, UK government backing.
  • Disadvantage Analysis: Few Chinese emission factors, domestic businesses need manual supplementation; Medium supply chain data collection capability, relying on database queries; Higher pricing, small business subscription burden.
  • Pricing Structure: Basic version annual fee $5,000; Advanced version annual fee $15,000 (includes Scope 3); Certification connection service fee $3,000.

GHG Protocol Tools Carbon Accounting Software Details

GHG Protocol Tools is the official carbon accounting tool launched by the World Resources Institute (WRI), emphasizing GHG Protocol standard compliance.

  • Core Capabilities: GHG Protocol accounting standard official tool; Scope 1, Scope 2 accounting mainly, Scope 3 requires additional templates; Excel template data entry, simplified accounting process; Official emission factor library covering mainstream energy types.
  • Technical Advantages: GHG Protocol official authority, highest standard compliance; Completely free use, small business zero cost; Simple Excel templates, low learning cost; Clear accounting logic, suitable for beginners.
  • Disadvantage Analysis: Limited Scope 3 accounting coverage, requires additional templates; Lacks API integration, data entry relies on Excel; No certification support function, accounting data cannot be used for certification applications; No cloud platform, data storage locally managed.
  • Pricing Structure: Completely free use; No subscription fees; Suitable for budget-constrained small businesses.

Emitwise Carbon Accounting Platform Details

Emitwise is an emerging carbon accounting platform that emphasizes Scope 3 supply chain penetration and China emission factor support.

  • Core Capabilities: ISO14064+GHG Protocol accounting standards; Scope 1, Scope 2, Scope 3 complete coverage; API integration with ERP systems+supply chain questionnaire collection; Global+China emission factor library, domestic business friendly; Carbon neutral certification connection, supports CDP, GRI report generation.
  • Technical Advantages: Leading supply chain collection, supplier questionnaire for Scope 3 data; Rich China emission factors covering domestic energy types; Strong API integration automation, reducing manual entry; Chinese interface support, domestic business friendly.
  • Disadvantage Analysis: Emerging platform, lower brand recognition; Longer supply chain questionnaire collection cycle, requires supplier cooperation; Higher pricing than free tools, small businesses need budget investment.
  • Pricing Structure: Basic version annual fee $8,000; Advanced version annual fee $20,000 (includes supply chain collection); Certification connection service fee $2,000.

Carbon Trust certification needCarbon TrustMature certification connection$5,000-15,000

Domestic small businessesEmitwiseChinese interface+China emission factors$8,000
Beginner entryGHG Protocol ToolsFree+standard authority$0 free

2026 Carbon Accounting Software Trend Predictions

In 2026, the carbon accounting software market will show three trends: ISSB standard integration with the International Sustainability Standards Board standard becoming the unified accounting framework; Real-time accounting adoption with IoT energy consumption data real-time collection and real-time accounting becoming standard; Blockchain data storage with carbon emission data on-chain storage ensuring accounting results cannot be tampered with. Small businesses should prioritize platforms supporting ISSB standard integration and real-time accounting to meet future regulatory requirements.
author: Carbon Management Consultant
